videos.title: Daily Routines English Conversations for English Speaking Practice | English Story for Speaking

lines.text: English speaking course. Meeting each other for the first time.
Hi. Is this Amber Brown's house?
Yep, that's me. Please come in. A Bella.
Nice to meet you. Thanks. Nice to meet you. I'm Amber. Did you have a hard time finding here? I'm a bit new to this
area, so I got lost.
You'll get used to the area soon. Let me show you around first.
That would be great. So, this is the living room, the biggest room in the house. Wow, it's crazy huge.
Yep, it's big enough to throw a party.
All right, next is the kitchen. I love the decor. It feels so cozy in here. And check out the snacks.
Oh, snacks. You know the way to my heart.
Oh, I forgot about the garden. Wow, your garden looks so nice. I love homegrown food, so that's why. Girl, you met the
right person. I'll bring my own garden tools, too.
Seems like you came to the right place.
What is the Wi-Fi password, Amber? It's single and fabulous.
That's a cool password.
Yeah. And fun fact, I've just changed it recently.
Did something happen?
I just broke up with my boyfriend. He cheated on me with my old roommate.
What? You just broke up? You heard it right. And I kicked both of them out.
Yeah, they deserve it.
He was a total red flag and I just kept ignoring it. Bella, I feel you, Amber.
Love blinds us all. What about you, Bella? Are you single, too? Yeah, I'm happily single. My last ex was
ridiculous.
Spill the tea, sister.
So, we were together two years, and he was super clingy. He got jealous every time I went out with my friends.
Wow. He took the word love to a whole new level. And then guess what? I found out he'd been secretly messaging an
influencer on Instagram.
Like with the perfect body and sponsored protein shakes.
Yeah, he got bored of me really fast.
So yeah, I dumped him faster than yesterday's news.
Seriously, you deserve way better than that. Thanks, girl.
We are strong, independent women who need no men.
New roommate,
Lucy, Mark, what is going on here?
Oh, hi, Amber. We would just uh about to kiss each other. Babe, it's not what it looks like. Then what? Did you trip and
fall into each other's lips? Amber, I can explain. Explain what? You told me you want a break for a while and now
this? What a joke. You two have been cheating behind my back the whole time.
It's not like that, Amber. We didn't mean for this to happen.
Well, it did happen, Lucy. And I can't believe I trusted you both.
Amber, please. Can't we talk about this?
That's enough. Both of you get out of this house right now.
But Amber, please. No more excuses. Pack your stuff and leave.
I'm sorry, Amber. I never meant to hurt you.
Oh, please stop with that fake ugly crying. No, Amber, it's not. Goodbye, and I will never see you again.
Oh my god, Molly, you cannot believe what just happened.
What's wrong? Are you crying?
Yeah, I look super ugly right now.
Forget the mascara. Spill the tea. That jerk Mark cheated on me with my roommate.
See, I warned you about him before. He's a walking red flag. I can't believe Lucy fell for that jerk. Whatever. I kicked
both of them out. They deserve it. I need to find a new roommate. Perfect timing. My cousin Bella is looking for a
new place. She has to change her workplace. So, all right, then. Give me her number. I don't know how I could
find a roommate this quick. Perhaps the universe is sending help to you after this breakup.
You got to be kidding me. Thanks anyway, Molly.
Going to the grocery store.
Bella, let's go buy some groceries. The fridge is empty again. How far is the grocery store from here? It's just a few
blocks away. Great. I don't want any sore feet.
Oh, stop being lazy, Bella. I won't piggyback you even if you complain.
All right, first stop, veggies. What do we need? Let's grab some onions and tomatoes.
Look at those fresh bell peppers. Are they on sale? It is now the season, so you can get them at a cheaper price.
Really? How much are they? Only $2.20 per pound, ma'am. Great. I'll take three pound then. Could you please weigh them
for us?
They're three pound.
Thank you very much.
Amber, do we have enough sauce at home?
I'm not sure. Let's grab a jar just in case. What kind of sauce then?
Let's pick ketchup.
Do you like barbecue sauce for grilled meat? Absolutely. You should get one.
Speaking of which, let's hold a barbecue and invite the neighbors.
Your idea sounds great, Bella. The neighbors love having a barbecue.
What do you want to buy? Let's get some muffins. I'm craving them. The cookie section has a buy one get one free
offer.
How about some chocolate chip cookies, too? Deal.
All right, I think we've got everything we need.
Is that all for you today?
>> Yes, thank you. Just these items today.
How much are they in total?
Your total is $35.
Let me pay for it, Amber. You got the last round of drinks.
Ah, you're the best, Bella.
Cooking together.
This smells amazing, Amber. What spices did you use?
Just a mix of cumin and a little paprika for a kick. Smells delicious. I haven't cooked anything this good in ages.
Takeout life is getting old.
My old roommate wouldn't even boil an egg. That Lucy girl? Seriously, she's that bad?
Yeah, she lived on cereal and delivery apps. Zero kitchen skills.
Wow. Must have been a nightmare.
It was. And I'm so happy we can cook together. Me, too. Plus, it's cheaper than takeout and it's way more
rewarding.
Wait, how do you chop these onions?
Just dice them into small pieces.
Great. Now I'm going to cry for no reason.
You can do it, Bella. It only stings for a little bit. Crying after a breakup is still better than this.
Oh, come on. You've got to be kidding me, Bella.
I'm sorry. I just find it funny. Have you finished crying, Princess? Can you pass me the sugar jar?
Here you go. Don't put too much sugar in it, Amber. It'll taste disgusting.
I know. Don't worry. I have so much experience cooking this dish. Maybe we should try different cuisines for each
week. I'm in Mexican next week.
Challenge accepted.
Bella, can you do the taste test for me?
It needs a little more salt, but still it's good. You know, your ex didn't know how to cook this. True. He didn't know
how to appreciate it either.
at the bar.
So, tell me about your parents. They're workaholics, right?
Yeah. They built a successful company together. Wow. That's impressive. Must be nice having successful parents.
It's not what you'd think. They're busy most of the time. Where are they now?
They live abroad. We only gather together on the holidays.
Wow, that's sad. But that's their choice after all. They chose career over me.
Ouch, that sucks. But hey, at least you have me now. I know. It's much better to have you by my side. We're supposed to
have fun tonight. Cheers.
So, have you tried the new cocktail here? You mean the electric blue unicorn? Of course I have. How's it
taste? It tastes like a rainbow in a glass.
Sounds cool. I might give it a try. You should. Anyway, did you hear about my sister Susan's promotion?
Susan, what happened with her? She got promoted to manager.
Wonderful. She must be thrilled. I'm so proud of her. She worked really hard for it. She's dating Tom from accounting
now. Come on, that's obvious. Everyone knows Tom is perfect for Susan.
Yeah, they make a cute couple.
Well, speaking of couple, look who just came in.
My god, I've tried to avoid them the entire month. Why do they have to show up? Well, if you don't feel good, we can
leave. Too late. They already spotted us. Don't worry, we got this. Okay, this is going to be fun.
Hey, Amber. Didn't expect to see you here. What a small world.
Hi there. I'm Bella, Amber's roommate.
So, you finally found a replacement, huh? It must be hard to find someone who can tolerate you.
Oh, Amber's amazing. We have so much fun together, unlike some people I know.
So, uh, what are you guys drinking?
Maybe we can join you. Thanks, but we're good. We don't need pity drinks.
Yeah, besides cocktails here are not the best quality. You two deserve better, right?
Fine, suit yourselves. Come on, Mark.
Let's get out of here.
Oh my gosh, that was hilarious.
You should have seen their faces.
Thank you for helping me, Bella. High five.
Job interview.
Welcome, Bella. I'm Steve Phillips, the personnel director. It's my pleasure to meet you, Steve. Please have a seat.
Let's start with your experience. Can you tell me about it?
I have three years of experience in social media marketing. I've worked for many international media campaigns. I
develop and analyze marketing strategies for promoting brands and services.
Impressive. What skills can you bring to the table?
I'm great at communication and I love working on teams.
All right. Why do you want to work here?
I admire the company's achievements and status. That's why I want to upgrade my skills while working here.
Okay, last question. Why should we hire you? I have great motivation and I'm willing to take on new challenges.
Thank you, Bella. We'll be in touch soon. Thank you for the opportunity.
Have a great day.
So, how did the interview go?
I was nervous at first, but then things went well. That's great to hear. Did anything interesting happened? Well, I
accidentally spilled water on the desk.
Oh, no. That is so embarrassing.
Yeah, but he laughed it off. It broke the tension, thankfully.
How do you feel about the interview?
Honestly, I feel good about it. I answered all of his questions. Well, that's awesome, Bella. I'm proud of you.
Thanks, Susan. I really want this job.
And you know what made me feel good, too. It's the guy who interviewed me. Do you have a crush on him? Maybe. He was
so handsome and gentle and he gave me his number after the interview.
What? Today must be a lucky day for you.
I have a feeling you'll get the job.
Positive vibes all the way. Thanks for the support, Susan. It means a lot.
A trip to Paris.
Bella, can you believe we're actually in Paris?
I know, right? It feels like a dream.
First things first, got to get a photo under the Eiffel Tower.
Relax. We have so much time here.
>> Excuse me. Oh my gosh. I'm so sorry.
We're just so excited to be here. We're tourists from America. Oh, no worries.
Enjoy your time in Paris.
Thank you. Have a good day.
Now, where is the bakery?
Hello. You look lost. Can I help you with anything?
We're trying to find a good bakery for breakfast. Do you have any recommendations?
Of course. There's a wonderful little place just around the corner called Panda E Chocolate.
They have the best pastries in Paris, and the owner of that is a lovely lady.
Thank you so much.
>> This place looks amazing. What should I choose? There are so many good options.
I don't know, but we should be quick.
This place is getting crowded.
Hello. May I have an almond croissant and a box of macarons?
Okay, your total cost is 15.
We should definitely come back here again. The taste here is no joke.
Trouble at work.
Ah, this is the worst.
What's wrong, AM? Did you stay up all night? You could say that. I just got the worst email from Mr. Jones
yesterday.
Mr. Jones, what happened? I worked on the presentation slides for the whole week. I tried to make sure everything
was perfect, but he said they were a total disaster.
Wow, that's harsh. Did he say what was wrong with them? He didn't say it clearly. And that's the problem. I
wonder what is wrong with him lately.
I got the news, guys.
His son isn't doing well at school and his wife is cheating on him.
Really? That's terrible.
Great. Now I'm his first victim.
At least you have time to fix it, right?
I guess. It's just so much pressure.
I really wanted to make him proud with this project.
Don't be so hard on yourself, Amber. Mr.
Jones can be a bit of a perfectionist sometimes.
I know. I know. It's just frustrating.
Maybe we could look at the slides together.
Really, you'd do that? Absolutely. Three heads are better than one, right? I don't know what I'd do without you.
Don't mention it, Amber. We've all been there. Thanks again you guys. I owe you one.
New client.
Good morning. How can I help you? I need help with my new project. Sure thing.
But first, can I have your name, please?
My name's Emily.
Nice to meet you, Emily. I'm Amber.
Let's discuss your project's details.
I'm looking to expand my business online.
That's exciting. What do you offer? We provide handmade accessories.
All right. Do you have a website? No, I'm starting from scratch.
We can design a website for you. That would be great.
Could you share your vision for the website? I'm thinking of a modern design with easy navigation.
All right. Any specific colors or themes? It will have blue and silver for elegance.
We'll create a design that fits your request. I suggest you could add customer feedback.
Real reviews will build trust among customers. That's a good suggestion.
I will schedule another meeting for further discussion.
Okay. How about Tuesday next week? Yes, that will work. Thank you for joining me today. I'm looking forward to our next
meeting.
Ordering food.
Oh, I'm starving. What will we have for dinner?
We're fresh out of groceries. Should we order food for a change?
Why not? I'm too lazy to cook anyway.
Let's see. How about pizza? I'm craving some cheese.
Hold on. Didn't we have pizza last Friday? Let's go for other options.
How about sushi? It's light and healthy.
Really, Bella? It costs a lot of money to begin with and it doesn't fill up my stomach at all. We've been going organic
for so long. Let's consider today a cheat day. That's tempting, but no thanks. I'm fed up with pizza dough.
Then how about Chinese food? H Chinese food?
H I remember the last time having a super spicy and oily tofu dish.
After that, I was up all night in the bathroom.
What? Your stomach must be so weak. But don't worry, there are healthier options, too. We could have steamed egg
or dumplings.
Sounds good. Do you want to order drinks, too? Of course. Any ideas besides milkshakes?
Let's get bubble tea tonight. I found coupons on their website.
All right, then.
We will order a smallsized pizza for me, dumplings for you, and finally two boba teas. Deal.
First day at work.
Good morning, Bella.
I'm Steve, the HR manager here. It's my pleasure to meet you.
Welcome to the first day in our company.
How are you today? I'm really excited to be here.
All right. For today, I will show you around the office and then I will introduce you to your colleagues. Is
that okay for you? Yes, of course. Okay.
Please come with me, Bella. Here is the canteen. You can visit it during your break time. Most of our employees dine
here. The food here is very tasty. Here is the company's biggest library. You can borrow books and buy drinks here as
well. It can be a really productive working space. That's awesome.
On the eighth floor, we have a gym. It has been built recently, but a lot of people have used it. You can come here
to have a quick workout during or after your work hours.
Now, if you don't have any questions, I'll take you to meet your colleagues.
Good morning, guys. I have an announcement to make today. Our team will have a new member.
Hello everyone. My name is Bella. Nice to see you all.
Welcome, Bella. I'm Bill, the leader of the team. Nice to have you on board.
So, what were you doing before joining our team? I was in the same position back in a company in California. The job
was great, but I had to move to a new state due to personal reasons.
>> I see. How long have you worked here?
>> I've been here for 3 years.
Good day, Bella. I'm Liz. Nice to meet you. I've been here for two years. Our team also has Mary, but today she's
absent. She would be really pleased to meet you.
Thank you for welcoming me, guys. I'm looking forward to working with you.
Weekend at home.
It's finally the weekend. Let's do something fun.
What should we do? We haven't planned anything for this weekend.
You said you wanted to throw a barbecue party and invite the neighbors. This weather is perfect for a barbecue.
Who are we inviting?
Well, the couple next door, the Millers, seem nice. And there's that friendly family with the golden retriever across
the street.
What about the Korean family down the block? They seem fun.
All right, next step. food.
Burgers and hot dogs are a must. And what about side dishes?
Potato salad, kleslaw, maybe some grilled veggies.
Let's get them fresh out of our garden.
Yeah, we'll save a lot of money.
Don't forget some refreshing drinks.
We got lemonade, iced tea, and some beer. We could ask everyone to bring a potluck dish.
That's smart, Amber. That will make things easier for us. And everyone loves a potluck, too.
We're home.
Did someone say barbecue?
Mom, Dad, why are you here? You're supposed to be working right now. We'll talk about work later because you are
more important than work. We want to spend time with you, sweetie.
Oh my god, you guys are so cheesy.
I missed you so much. I missed you guys, too.
We are so happy that you guys are here.
This is going to be the best barbecue ever.
Party night.
How do I look? I'm so nervous for tonight. You always look amazing, Amber.
Relax. Everyone's going to love you.
Besides, there are a lot of cool people there. Hopefully, maybe even some single cute guys.
Oh, come on. Admit it. You wouldn't mind meeting someone new. Trust me, you will like this guy. He's charming and sweet
at the same time. We will have so much fun tonight.
This is amazing, Bella. I can't remember the last time I had this much fun.
See, you're great in here. Hey, who are you looking at? The one from your workplace with curly hair and a wide
smile. Wow, Amber, you have an amazing memory.
And he's just too handsome to be true.
I'll take you over there so you guys can meet each other. So, that's the guy who will meet me tonight. You got it right,
Amber. He's single, so I want to help you take a chance.
Hey, Noah. This is Amber I told you about earlier. Amber, this is Noah.
Hey, Amber. Great to meet you. Bella didn't stop talking about how awesome you are. Thanks.
So, Amber, what do you think of the party? It's fantastic. I haven't had as much fun as I've had tonight. Do you
want to escape the dance floor for a minute? It's getting a little crowded in here. Sounds good to me. Mind if we go
away for a bit, Bella? Go for it. Have fun, guys.
You know, you're even more fun than Bella made you sound.
Thanks. I'm glad you're having a good time. So, Amber, what are you up to this weekend? Maybe we could grab a coffee.
Coffee sounds great. I'm down for it.
Actually, I'm free Saturday afternoon.
Sounds like a plan. I'll text you my number so you can confirm.
Awesome. Looking forward to it, Noah.
Shopping.
Omg, Susan, you won't believe this.
Calm down. What happened? I got a date.
Remember Steve, the guy who interviewed me? He asked me out.
That's amazing. What day is it? Saturday night. And that's the problem. My closet is a disaster zone. I have nothing to
wear.
Let's head to the mall, then.
Wow, this place is packed. Let's check the new arrival section over there. I'm sure all the trendy stuff is there.
Okay, Bella, look at this dress. It's perfect.
But is it a little sparkly?
Sparkle is good. It's a date night, right?
I'm not a fan of sparkly things. So, how about we check out that area? Maybe we'll have more discounts there.
All right. How do I look? Five out of 10. Blue looks so lame on you. But Steve loves blue.
You only look best in what you love wearing. You can't wear that just because that Steve guy likes it. Only
your personality matters.
Thanks for the TED talk. You're welcome.
Okay. How about this? You look stunning, Bella. Hi, ladies. Do you need help with anything? Yes, please. Could we get this
dress in a different size?
Absolutely. What size are you looking for? A smaller size, please. Noted. I'll be right back.
Rate this look, Susan.
This is your best so far. I will go with this one for the date. How much is this?
That dress is currently on sale for $75.
Okay, I'll take it.
New project.
All right, team. Today we will brainstorm for the upcoming campaign for our new fitness tracker.
Sounds exciting.
Who are we aiming at?
We will target young adults who want to stay fit.
So the message must sound energetic, right?
It's something young people can connect with.
Maybe we could use social media influencers. They have a lot of reach with our target audience.
That's a smart suggestion, Bella.
Now, let's talk about where to advertise.
I would suggest using Instagram and Tik Tok. Lots of young people use them for fitness motivation.
What if we create funny skits on Tik Tok to introduce the tracker? By that way, we could attract younger customers.
I agree. Everyone falls for entertainment.
What about a catchy slogan for the campaign?
How about track your way to fitness?
Or maybe unlock your potential with the tracker's name included. That sounds more powerful.
Those are both great options. We will fix them later.
Now, any ideas for offline media channels.
What about college campuses? We could set up booths with free trials and contests.
We could also give free goodies with every track or purchase. Everyone loves free stuff, right? That's right.
Freebies always attract attention. This is so productive, guys.
We've had so many good ideas already.
We will take a small break before the next discussion.
Let's go get some coffee, guys.
Don't forget to practice your English every day to improve your English level.
Watch the video for one week.
Try to think and speak in English every time.
Listen and repeat the lesson out loud to improve your listening and speaking skills fast. Thanks for watching. Please
give us a like, share, and comment.
Click here for more useful videos.
English speaking course.
Your failure isn't her fault.
You got into fashion school months ago, Ellie.
Wait, what's happening here? What led them to argue like that? Maybe we should go back to the beginning. That's a good
idea.
Your failure isn't her fault.
Guys, I got in.
I got into Rochester University engineering.
I knew it. I knew you could do it, Lucy.
Can you believe it? I'll be in those modern labs learning from the best professors.
Wow, great, Lucy. I'm really happy for you.
Jenny, you okay? Why do you sound Oh, don't worry about me.
I didn't get accepted. I found out last night, actually.
So, I guess my dream is over.
Don't say that, Jenny. And of course I'm worried about you.
You're my best friend.
Best friends don't leave each other behind.
We promised to go to New York together.
Maybe if I hadn't spent so much time helping you study.
What are you talking about, Jenny?
You chose to help her. That was your decision.
Your failure isn't her fault.
You got into fashion school months ago, Ellie. You don't know how this feels.
I'm so sorry you didn't get in, Jenny.
I didn't know you felt this way. Maybe I shouldn't have You don't have to apologize to her,
Lucy.
You studied hard for this.
Oh, right. You don't have to be sorry, Lucy.
Just go enjoy your perfect dream.
She is really hurt, Ellie.
She said I'm leaving her behind. I broke our promise.
Listen to me, Lucy.
A good friend helps you move forward, not try to stop you.
But what Jenny is doing isn't supporting you. She wants you to fail with her.
She's been my friend for so long.
I know, and everyone in your life is good for you.
Sometimes the best choice is to walk away from a relationship that holds you back.
You are right, Ellie. Thank you.
We knew you'd make it.
Dude, you've been staring at that coffee for 10 minutes. What's up?
It's my review meeting today with Ms. Davis. It's in 15 minutes.
Oh, that. Relax. Everyone has to go through it.
You'll be fine.
How? What if she asks about the Jenkins file?
The one I sent wrong last month?
Okay, rule number one, just be honest.
Say you made a mistake. No excuses.
Yes, I apologize for that. There was a mix-up and I sent the wrong report.
And don't stop there. Tell her what you learned from it.
Like?
After that, I always double-check my work with the team before I send it.
It shows you're serious about your job.
Be honest, then talk about the lesson.
One more thing, don't sound like you're asking to stay.
Then what should I sound like?
Like someone who already belongs here.
Say you see a future here and you want to help the company grow.
Okay, I can do this. Thanks, guys.
So, Alex, how do you feel about your first 3 months here?
It's been good. Of course, there were some challenges, but I learned from each one.
You meant the client report?
Yes, that one.
The good thing was the team helped me fix it right away.
We even created a quick checklist so it won't happen again.
That's good to hear.
And looking ahead, where do you see yourself?
Well, I really enjoy being here.
I like the team and the work we do.
I'd love to stay and help the company grow.
Mhm. I'm happy to tell you the review went well.
You passed, Alex.
Hey, guys. I passed.
Woah.
Welcome to the team, officially. We knew you'd make it.
>> [music] >> Are you guys together now?
So, don't make me wait.
How was the date with Dr. Dreamy Martin?
It was perfect.
Seriously.
Details. I need details.
We met in the morning and got coffee.
Then we went for a walk in the park.
Aw.
We were just talking and he actually listened.
Oh, Allison.
Later on, we went to see a movie. That new romantic comedy everyone's talking about.
And?
And for dinner, he took me to this Italian place with candles on every table.
It was so romantic.
He was really nice and funny.
This is it. He's the one, right?
Are you guys together now? When's the next date?
No, it's not going to happen.
Why not?
You said it was perfect.
Because they are not okay with it.
They? Who's they?
You mean his parents? Are they Not his parents.
Then who? His friends? His pet?
Tell me. I'll fight them.
His wife and his 4-year-old son.
Want to speak English naturally, just like in these stories?
Join our membership and you'll get Learn English with fun stickers, full story PDFs, and Quizlet sets
to build your vocabulary step by step.
Join now and learn real English you can actually use every day.
He was my savior.
I still can't believe your parents let you have a dog.
Weren't they super against pet hair in the house?
Oh, they were. No pets, too much mess.
But Buddy here, he was my savior.
Your savior? How?
When I was seven, I walked through this park every day.
And I'd see him here all skinny.
I started giving him some of my food.
Your parents found out, right?
Yeah, they were very mad, so I stopped going to the park for a while.
So, what changed?
My parents took me to a festival there.
It was crowded and I got lost.
Then a man came up to me [music] and asked me to go with him.
Yo, dude.
Out of nowhere Buddy appeared. He started barking loudly at the man.
The man got scared and quickly ran away.
Not all heroes wear capes, huh, Buddy?
And he stayed right by my side until my parents found us.
And after that, Buddy became family?
Yep. Now my parents always call him David's bodyguard.
It's just a bad cold.
My dear sister, if I don't make it, tell Mom and Dad I love them.
You have a cold, Mark. Stop being such a drama queen.
It's not a cold. It's the plague. Aren't you in medical school?
Do something.
Fine. Tell me what's wrong.
But talk like a normal patient.
My head, it's boom boom boom.
Your head is pounding. That's a headache. What else?
My nose is like a waterfall.
So, a runny nose. Noted.
And my eyes, they're all wet and sad.
Watery eyes, normal with a cold.
Anything else? Cough? sore throat?
No. Headache, runny nose, watery eyes.
That's a full body attack.
Again, it's just a bad cold.
All you need is soup, lots of water, and less complaining.
Worst doctor ever.
All right. I'll have my chicken soup by myself.
I mean, best sister ever. And one bowl of soup, no carrots, please.
Was anyone hurt?
Daniel, sweetie.
Mom? What's wrong? Something happened?
I just saw the news. There was a building on fire on your street. Are you all right?
Mom, calm down. I live in the building across the street. I'm safe here.
Oh, thank goodness. What happened?
They think the fire started from a gas leak in the apartment on the 15th floor.
A gas leak? That's so dangerous.
The fire was huge.
And I saw firefighters pull people to safety.
It took them nearly half an hour to put out the fire.
Oh dear. Was anyone hurt?
Two people were seriously hurt and four more were lightly burned.
Oh, those poor people.
Daniel, please promise me you'll be extra careful.
I promise, Mom. I will.
Remember to check the stove before you leave. And Mom, it's okay. My building has good smoke alarms and everything.
I know, but I'm your mother. Worrying is what I do.
Call me again tomorrow, all right?
I will. Love you, Mom.
>> [music]
>> Moving in.
>> [music]
